## XProvide() function

Generates a provide function that returns an object with the injectable value returned in a getter to keep its reactivity, using the default [Vue inject](https://vuejs.org/v2/api/#provide-inject)<!-- -->. It overrides the provide key if the parent provides the same key.

<b>Signature:</b>

```typescript
export declare function XProvide<Type>(provideKey: AnyInjectKey<Type>): DecoratorFor<Type>;
```

## Parameters

|  Parameter | Type | Description |
|  --- | --- | --- |
|  provideKey | AnyInjectKey&lt;Type&gt; | The key used to provide. This key can be a 'string' or a 'XInject<Type>'. This last type is to support type-safe injection. When this decorator is used, it is possible to specify the type of the injected value. Take a look to the example below. |

<b>Returns:</b>

[DecoratorFor](./x-components.decoratorfor.md)<!-- -->&lt;Type&gt;

Decorator with the provide configuration.

## Remarks

The decorated property needs to be public for type inference to work.

## Example

Type unsafe injection (but allowed): @<!-- -->XProvide('myKey')

Type safe injection (recommended): const myKey: XInjectKey<Date> = 'myKey'; @<!-- -->XProvide(myKey)

This last one, you are specifying that the injected value with the key 'myKey' has the Date type.
